steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The subject is the average processing power (singular). Strip out the nonessential clause "whose cores can number in the dozens, hundreds, or even thousands" — it modifies microchips, not the subject. The sentence also uses 'since then,' pointing to a span from the early 2000s to the present, which requires present-perfect tense.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "has improved": Singular present-perfect. Matches the singular subject and the since-then timeframe. ✓
  • "have improved": Plural present-perfect. Disagrees with the singular subject. ✗
  • "have been improving": Plural present-perfect progressive. Disagrees with the singular subject. ✗
  • "are improving": Plural simple present. Disagrees with the singular subject and does not capture the since-then span. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
